mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand growth that drives corporate earnings expectations higher, generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC greater than 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that boosts short-term market sentiment and yields positive weekly S&P 500 returns during economic recoveries.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns remain flat or negative despite sustained industrial production growth in early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250, persistent inflation forces monetary tightening expectations that compress valuation multiples and produce negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 70% of observations where CPIAUCSL remains above the 250 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust job creation that supports consumer spending and drives positive quarterly market performance.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward quarterly returns show zero or negative correlation with GDP growth across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in industrial production exceeding 1 percent signals severe supply-side contraction that triggers defensive portfolio positioning and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 60% of triggered instances despite the specified industrial output contraction.
